title: tulipa praecox subsp. raddii
intro: Tulipa praecox subsp. raddii, also known as Raddi's early tulip or wild tulip, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant or its preparations is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Tulipa praecox subsp. raddii include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
